▼ Cellular Automata
CONTROLS SHAPE
Hydra
Presets

Click any preset to load it. Code appears in the editor on the right. Hit Run (or Cmd+Shift+Enter) to execute.

Autoplay

Cycles through presets automatically. Set interval by time (seconds) or bars (synced to BPM). Great for ambient museum loops.

Global Controls

Speed scales animation time. Hue / Saturation / Brightness / Contrast are post-processing on the final output. Sharpen adds detail.

Editor

Live code editor. Select a block and hit Cmd+Enter to run just that selection. Full Hydra API — osc(), noise(), src(), etc.

Daydream / Scope
What This Does

Connects to Scope (Daydream's AI video engine) via NDI. The AI feed comes in on s3 and can be blended with Hydra visuals.

NDI Virtual Input

If NDI Virtual Input doesn't show the Scope source in its dropdown:

  • The NDI system extension can linger after quitting the app
  • Find it: ps aux | grep -i ndi
  • Kill the .systemextension process: sudo kill <PID>
  • Reopen NDI Virtual Input — it respawns fresh

Startup choppiness is normal — NDI needs a few seconds to negotiate bandwidth after a fresh connection.

Notes Button

The Notes button opens a scratchpad for LoRA URLs, pipeline configs, or anything you want to remember between sessions. Saved to browser storage.

Cellular Automata
What This Is

GPU-accelerated reaction-diffusion simulation rendered on s0. Creates organic, evolving textures — coral, worms, mitosis patterns.

Controls
  • Hue / Brightness — color the output
  • Speed — simulation steps per frame
  • Thickness — line weight of the structures
  • Growth / Tolerance — reaction-diffusion feed/kill rates. Small changes = big visual shifts
  • Smoothness — blur passes between steps
Tips

CA writes to s0, so Hydra presets that use src(s0) will pick it up automatically. Hit Reseed to restart the simulation with fresh noise.
